meta.description: AI QA Platform — Heuristic keyword rules for risk scoring and automation suitability

automation_rules.reason: Requires live OTP from SMS/email which cannot be intercepted in automation
CAPTCHA is specifically designed to block automation tools
Biometric authentication requires real physical sensors
Requires real physical hardware that automation frameworks cannot simulate
Requires human visual judgment that cannot be reliably automated
Audio/voice interactions require specialized hardware and subjective evaluation
AR/VR requires specialized headsets and physical environment that cannot be automated
Usability testing requires real human participants and subjective judgment
Physical location-dependent testing cannot be replicated by automation
Physical device compatibility and durability cannot be automated
Legal and compliance reviews require human expert judgment
Exploratory testing is inherently human-driven and unscripted
Notification delivery verification requires external mailbox or device access
External service dependencies introduce flakiness and sandbox limitations
Dynamic/real-time data makes deterministic assertions challenging
File operations can be automated but browser security restrictions add complexity
Mobile gestures can be automated in emulators but real device testing is preferred
Blockchain interactions need specialized Web3 test frameworks and testnets
Document content can be partially validated but layout verification needs visual tools
Performance testing requires specialized tools and baseline establishment
Social media APIs are rate-limited and platform behavior varies
AI/ML outputs are probabilistic and require threshold-based validation
Map UI automation is possible but visual accuracy needs manual spot-checks
Real-time collaboration requires multi-client test setup which adds complexity
Streaming quality validation requires specialized media analysis tools
Automated accessibility tools catch ~30% of issues; manual testing still required
Email rendering varies by client; automated checks cover limited scenarios
Localization automation covers string presence but cultural/contextual review needs humans
IoT automation requires device simulators and may miss real hardware edge cases
Chatbot testing can be scripted but natural language variation requires broad test sets
Print layout can be partially validated via PDF comparison tools
API testing is the most automatable type — deterministic, fast, and reliable
Database validation is highly automatable with direct query assertions
Authentication flows are well-structured and stable automation candidates
Form interactions are stable and straightforwardly automatable
Navigation and routing behavior is deterministic and easily automatable
CRUD operations follow predictable patterns ideal for automation
Validation and error message assertions are deterministic and automatable
Search and filter functionality produces consistent results ideal for automation
Data display components have clear state transitions automatable with assertions
Role and permission checks have binary pass/fail criteria ideal for automation
Settings pages follow stable patterns easily covered by automation
Wizard and multi-step flows are linear and well-suited for automation
Notification state management has clear UI assertions for automation
Security headers can be validated automatically via API response inspection
Smoke and sanity tests are prime automation candidates for CI/CD pipelines
Regression tests for stable features are the core use case for automation
Batch and scheduled job outcomes are verifiable via API or database assertions
Email content validation via test mailboxes (Mailhog/Mailtrap) is automatable
Cache behavior can be validated via HTTP response header assertions
Post-deployment verification tests are essential and highly automatable
Translation key presence and fallback can be validated automatically
Automated accessibility scanners catch structural issues reliably
GraphQL operations are fully automatable via query assertions
Token lifecycle flows are deterministic and automatable
Feature flag states produce deterministic UI/API behavior automatable via assertions
Webhook and event payloads can be validated with request inspection tools
Data export content and format can be validated programmatically
Admin panel actions follow CRUD patterns that are ideal for automation
Cookie and storage behavior can be validated via browser automation assertions
HTTP status codes and headers are machine-readable and highly automatable
SEO attributes in HTML are extractable and assertable via automation
Password policy rules produce clear pass/fail UI states ideal for automation
Pagination logic and state are deterministic and automatable
Invitation and team management flows follow stable patterns for automation
Subscription state transitions are deterministic and automatable
Audit logs have structured entries with assertable fields
String constraint validation is deterministic and automatable
